title: Executive Order 11857—Amending Executive Order Nos. 11803, 11837, and 11842 to Provide Authority to Increase the Number of Members of the Presidential Clemency Board

flags.justification: This expands the President's discretion to add board members as needed, an expansion of capacity rather than a concentration of decision-making in fewer hands.
A board-membership amendment, not a compliance regime.
No compliance regime is created by this order.
The underlying Presidential Clemency Board's own termination provisions are unaffected by this narrow membership amendment.
No existing oversight mechanism is removed.
No compliance regime is created by this order.
A narrow procedural amendment (board size) to an already-established program; the amendment itself claims no new substantive authority.
No second agency's competing mandate is implicated.
No coverage population or exemption structure is created.

notes: Validation batch (negative). Structural weight: 0/10 = 0.0%. A narrow membership-size amendment to the Presidential Clemency Board, part of President Ford's historically significant Vietnam-era clemency program for draft evaders and military deserters, though this specific amendment does not touch the program's substantive standards.
